draft.introduction: Fragment 90 articulates Heraclitus’s doctrine of cosmic exchange, in which all things are traded for fire and fire for all things. This metaphor of universal currency grounds his physics in a single transformative substance.

translation.original_ref: DK 22 B90 (Plutarch, De E apud Delphos 388D–E)
translation.text: All things are an exchange for fire, and fire for all things, as goods are for gold and gold for goods.
translation.original_text: πυρός τε ἀνταμοιβὴ τὰ πάντα καὶ πυρὸς ἅπαντα, ὥσπερ χρυσοῦ χρήματα καὶ χρημάτων χρυσός

translator_notes.note: The gold-goods analogy illustrates that fire is not merely one element among others but the universal medium of transformation. The Stoics later developed this into their doctrine of ekpyrosis, the periodic conflagration and renewal of the cosmos.
